programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors had been learning about the evidence against the defendant, Ali Abulaban. He was accused of the premeditated murder of his estranged wife, Ana, and her friend Rayburn Barron. Both were shot in the face at close range. A close friend of Ana Abulaban took the stand. He had insights in the marital drama of the Abulabans.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: According to her friend, physical, mental, and verbal abuse had been a part of Ana Abulaban's home life with her husband before she died. Jurors would learn more about the domestic violence allegations in this case, which the state claimed led to the death of Ana and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Julia Stuntz's emotional testimony elaborated on the physical and emotional abuse her friend endured in her marriage.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The jury learned more about the issues in the relationship between the defendant and his wife, Ana. Months before she and her friend Rayburn Barron were killed, she was allegedly beaten, harassed, and even stalked by Ali. And she was forced to go to the police several times. The defense had questions for his wife's best friend. Julia Stuntz knew all about the drama between the two which included allegations of him trashing the apartment after he was kicked out, spying on her, and stalking her.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Another close friend of Ana Abulaban took the stand. Prosecutors wanted to portray the defendant, Ali Abulaban, as an abusive, jealous, out-of-control husband whose behavior led him to plot and kill his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Jurors heard from not one, but two close friends of Ana's who testified about the bruises on her face and arms allegedly caused by her estranged husband, Ali.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The allegations of abuse were crucial to the State's case against TikTok star-turned murder suspect Ali Abulaban. Before he was accused of shooting his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron, Ali was allegedly abusive toward Ana before their eventual separation. According to Ana's friends, she wanted to leave her husband. But she felt that she couldn't because divorce is discouraged in Filipino culture. Also, if she had divorced him, she could be deported, leaving her daughter with Ali.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learn about the physical abuse allegations against Ali Abulaban, along with accusations.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Friends of Ana Abulaban told jurors in her husband's murder trial that she kept giving Ali second chances. This was after they said she had bruises and faced psychological torment. For Ana's close friends, the signs were there that the violence she allegedly experienced was only going to get worse. But she hesitated in ending her marriage. Why? They said she was concerned about her daughter not having her father around, her U.S. citizenship status and the fact that she still loved Ali.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The defendant, Ali Abulaban, met another woman in the elevator of his apartment building. Ashley Sanchez testified that she had sex with him just weeks before the fatal shootings of his wife Ana and her friend, Rayburn Barron. When Ana Abulaban learned about Ali having sex with a random stranger, allegedly that was it for her. She wanted him gone, but eventually she would be the one who end up dead.

programme.desc: In Boston, FBI agents tracked down a gang of robbers who specialized in armored car takedowns. As the robberies grew increasingly more violent, authorities launched a complex sting to trap the deadly ring of thieves.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Richmond, Virginia, two masked men who were robbing a bank escaped after opening fire on officers responding to the call. Agents found one bank teller dead, another wounded; the bank's security guard had been shot four times yet was still alive.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.desc: In 1987, Cuban prisoners at the Atlanta Federal Prison broke out homemade weapons and stole from prison staff.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: For Tammy Brannen, Christmas was always a special time until her daughter was gone without warning in 1989.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, the FBI and the NYPD began a joint investigation into gas station mogul Gurmeet Dhinsa for gas pump rigging. However, they soon realized Dhinsa ruled his mob-like operation with brutal force, kidnapping and killing wayward employees.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an 8-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood. Within hours, police began to suspect a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children.

programme.desc: Musician and special needs teacher Jane Longhurst vanished during her day off work after making a plan to go swimming with her friend's boyfriend. Five weeks later, Jane's partially decomposed body was found burning 25 miles from Jane's home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert gained trust easily, slipping into the lives of vulnerable women and girls across countries. For decades, he manipulated, stalked and attacked, leaving a trail of victims in his wake until investigators finally exposed his pattern.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: The story of the Baton Rouge Serial Killer, known for murdering seven women in the area and sentenced to death for his chilling crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For more than a year, hateful letters were sent to a school teacher in Pennsylvania. DNA analysis would eventually help seal the perpetrator's fate.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1985, 121 people in South Dakota and Minnesota were struck by a mysterious illness. There had been only one outbreak like it, and when it happened again no one could figure out why.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. When her abandoned car is found, the tank is empty and a gas can she kept in her trunk for emergencies is missing.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Foul play is suspected when Fort Worth factory worker Glenda Furch disappears after completing her shift.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man's story of a killing contradicted physical evidence, investigators turned to forensic science.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession. A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Employing a range of forensic techniques, investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 1996 investigation into 48-year-old Martha Hansen's murder is helped by a legal requirement that bars have video cameras installed on the premises.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young boy went missing, police organized the largest search in the history of their small town.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: With no forensic evidence inside a murder scene, investigators were baffled. But they suspected that the victim's dog had witnessed the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Stephen Scher and Martin Dillon went skeet shooting on a beautiful spring day and Martin was shot dead.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The wife of a murdered horse rancher explains that the last time she saw her husband was when he was leaving the house to go jogging. Police become suspicious when forensic evidence eventually leads investigators back to the ranch.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives search for the bombers of two churches in Illinois, hoping that the materials used in the remnants of the handmade bombs will offer the clues to catch the culprits.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why a scientist is gravely ill. When tests reveal the cause, it's too late to save him.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, Patrick McRae was found dead in his Des Moines home and the crime scene was awash with blood.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are able to create a composite sketch of a man burnt beyond recognition. But tracking the killer is more challenging.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is found dead from a gunshot wound and police suspect her husband, however, forensic teams prove what really happened, and the truth is stranger than fiction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police find evidence that would prove what had happened and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home and walked up the stairs to the bedroom. She discovered her husband lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered...but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One investigator's quick thinking helps him capture a shoe impression made in snow before it melts away.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Grand Junction, Colorado is on edge after a series of deadly bombings kill two people and injure one more. Police race to find the culprit before he strikes again.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An 82-year-old woman was found dead. Clues on the victim's body would tell police what happened that night.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After inspecting storm damage to a home in Tampa, an insurance assessor simply disappeared. Thirty hours later, her body was found in a nearby river. But the killer had been careless.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a college co-ed was found on a farm covered with her own denim as well as the jeans of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
